observations.recommendation: Management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in order we can insure the Municipal Buildings in accordance with Republic Act No. 656, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.
observations.managementAction: We will communicate with GSIS on the required documents in order we can insure the Municipal Buildings.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
a. We reiterated our previous year audit recommendations for the LGU
to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and
equipment. Instruct the Municipal GSO and Accountant to
reconcile the records with the physical count. Items that are beyond
economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the
books. Management must adhere the PPSAS 17.
b. Management must create an Inventory Committee to take charge in
the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports
to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
c. Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on
all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual
existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current
condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities.
d. General Service Office must maintain complete PCs by providing
identification number, issued acknowledgement receipt for
equipment (ARE) and stickers to each property newly purchased or
acquired
e. Accounting Office to prepare PPELCs and update them periodically
to aid in the conduct of Physical Inventory Count and in the
reconciliation of balances between the Inventory Report and the
books of accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Assessor to prepare and submit the taxpayer’s assessment roll to the
Municipal Treasurer for use in the preparation of duly certified list of
taxpayers and the amount due thereon to be submitted to the Municipal
Accountant. Then the Municipal Accountant shall set-up, thru the
preparation of JEV at the beginning of the year the estimated receivable of
RPT and SET.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Accountant and Treasurer be instructed
to strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial
statements and DVs and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the
accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting
personnel, so as to prevent work overload, meet deadlines on reports
submission and to ensure ample time for the audit and also to provide the
agency of the needed information which could be vital in their decision
making.

Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their
transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting
documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the
regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should require the Municipal
Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require
insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS.

Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of
loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic
Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by
PD No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

We recommended that Management adhere to the pertinent provisions of the Local Government Code and encourage its Sangguniang Bayan to enact updated ordinances that can be the basis of the general revision of real property assessment and classification once every three (3) to maximize LGU’s effort in raising local revenues.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 will conduct information drive to encourage its constituent to be more social responsible in paying their real property taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommend Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A(1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Recommendation:
a. We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic
physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment. Items that are
beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from
the books. Management must adhere the PPSAS 17 & 27 of Volume I
and II respectively.
b. Management must create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the
overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the
office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
c. Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all
PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of
the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well
as pinpoint custodial responsibilities.
d. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment
Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property
records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able
to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial
statements.
e. Require the accountable officers of the LGU to update the physical
inventory of PPE in adherence to Section 63, Manual on the NGAS,
Vol. II and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE
(RPCPPE) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
f. Every accountable officer must adhere and carefully evaluate their
accountability on Section 494, GAAM, Vol. I, which states that “Every
accountable officer for government property shall be liable for its
money value in case of improper or unauthorized use or misapplication
thereof, by himself or by any person for whose acts he may be
responsible. He shall likewise be liable for all losses, damage or
deterioration occasioned by negligence in the keeping or use of the
property, whether or not it be at the time in his actual custody (PD
1445).

Recommendation:
We recommend that copies of POs/Contracts and AIR be submitted on time to
the Auditor as required under Section A(1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and
Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Accountant and Treasurer be instructed
to strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial
statements and DVs and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the
accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting
personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports
submission.

Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their
transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting
documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the
regulations.

Request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned
personnel until such time that the requirements of the COA are being
complied with Section 122(2), P.D. 1445.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should require the Municipal
Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require
insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS.

Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss
due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No.
656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No.
245 dated July 13, 1973.

We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.

The management should instruct concerned officials to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2), P.D. 1445).

We recommend that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor as required under Section A(1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

observations.recommendation: Management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment.  Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.

LGU must submit and furnished copies of contract and memorandum of agreement  and their required attachment within five (5) days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

The management should instruct officials concerned to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month.  Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2), P.D. 1445.

We recommend that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A(1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) fast tract the implementation of the projects/programs funded by the National Government Agencies so as not deprive further the public of the use and benefits derived from the said projects and follow-up to the concerned NGAs the immediate release of funds.

We recommended that management prepare its 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan with emphasis on the compliance of the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. Prepare annually the action plan on solid waste management that details the priority activities to be performed and delivered for the gradual implementation of its 10-year plan.

We recommended that management prepare its Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) and Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) which may lead to unfavourable impact socio-economic and environmental  development as provided under Section 106, 107 & 109  of R.A No. 7160, Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that to provide information for a complete profile of the Leagues of Local Government Units (LGUs) and the Leagues and Federations of Local Elective Officials pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2011-014 dated December 13, 2011 and COA Memorandum No. 2012-008.

We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment.  Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.

LGU must submit and furnished copies of contract and memorandum of agreement  and their required attachment within five (5) days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A(1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

The management should instruct officials concerned to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month.  Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2), P.D. 1445.

Management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

LGU must submit and furnished copies of contract and memorandum of agreement  and their required attachment within five (5) days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment.  Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books. Management must adhere the PPSAS 17 & 27 of Volume I and II respectively.
Management must create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities.
Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
Require the accountable officers of the LGU to update the physical inventory of PPE in adherence to Section 63, Manual on the NGAS, Vol.II and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
Every accountable officer must adhere and carefully evaluate their accountability on Section 494,GAAM, Vol. I, which states that “Every accountable officer for government property shall be liable for its money value in case of improper or unauthorized use or misapplication thereof, by himself or by any person for whose acts he maybe responsible. He shall likewise be liable for all losses, damage or deterioration occasioned by negligence in the keeping or use of the property, whether or not, it be at the time in his actual custody (PD 1445).

We recommend that municipal accountant and the municipal treasurer be instructed to strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial statements and disbursement vouchers and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) fast tract the implementation of the projects/programs funded by the National Government Agencies so as not deprive further the public of the use and benefits derived from the said projects and follow-up to the concerned NGAs the immediate release of funds.

We recommended that management prepare its 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan with emphasis on the compliance of the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. Prepare annually the action plan on solid waste management that details the priority activities to be performed and delivered for the gradual implementation of its 10-year plan.

We recommended that management prepare its Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) and Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) which may lead to unfavorable impact socio-economic and environmental  development as provided under Section 106, 107 & 109  of R.A No. 7160, Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that to provide information for a complete profile of the Leagues of Local Government Units (LGUs) and the Leagues and Federations of Local Elective Officials pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2011-014 dated December 13, 2011 and COA Memorandum No. 2012-008.
